name: Cheese-Stuffed Chicken in Phyllo
recipeIngredient: 8 skinless boneless chicken breast halves (1 1/2 pounds total)
2 tablespoons olive oil or cooking oil
4 ounces cream cheese, cubed and softened
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound each chicken breast half between two sheets of heavy plastic wrap with the flat side of a meat mallet until it is 1/8 inch thick. Season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
2. In a large skillet, cook spinach and onion in hot oil until onion is tender. Remove from heat.
3. Stir in cream cheese until blended. Stir in remaining cheese, egg yolk, flour, nutmeg, and cumin.
4. Place about 1/4 cup of the spinach mixture on each chicken breast half. Roll up jelly-roll style. Do not seal ends.
5. Place one sheet of phyllo on a work surface. Keep the rest of the sheets covered with a damp towel.
6. Brush with some of the melted margarine or butter. Place another phyllo sheet on top of the first. Brush with margarine.
7. Place one chicken roll near the short side of the phyllo. Roll chicken and phyllo over once to cover chicken. Fold in long sides, continue rolling from the short side.
8. Repeat with the rest of the chicken, phyllo, and margarine.
9. Brush with margarine.
10. Bake, uncovered, in a 350°F oven for 30-35 minutes or until chicken is no longer pink.
